method_atom: particle-based molecular simulation of a dense array of tethered, mutually interacting flexible chains driven by a shear or pressure-gradient flow of an embedding medium, revealing that individual constituents undergo periodic tumbling with non-Gaussian fluctuations of their orientation; the collective coupling of these driven units to the surrounding fluid reverses the direction of near-boundary transport, an effect quantified as an effective linear slip boundary condition relating surface velocity to shear rate.
object_atom: a dense polymer brush of grafted, unentangled chains in contact with an explicit chemically-identical polymeric solvent, subjected to Couette and Poiseuille flow near the brush-coated surface.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the driven-tethered-units-with-cyclic-tumbling machinery producing near-wall flow inversion characterized by an effective slip law; the polymer-brush-in-solvent grafted-chain system is the domain-specific object.
